Just in: Goldman Sachs: If transportation in the Strait of Hormuz continues to be blocked, Brent oil may rise above $120 by the end of the year.

Goldman Sachs Group’s latest statement is that if supply disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz continue, Brent crude oil prices may rise to more than $120 per barrel in the fourth quarter.

Goldman Sachs analysts Persian Gulf and others wrote in a July 20 report that "escalating tensions in the Middle East and an expected decline in Persian Gulf oil flows to less than 45% of pre-war levels have contributed to a recovery in oil prices." Currently, Goldman Sachs expects Brent crude prices to be at $80 a barrel in the fourth quarter and $75 a barrel next year, assuming tensions in the Middle East ease.

However, analysts also noted that risks to these forecasts are "skewed to the upside" due to shipping disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz and possibly the Red Sea. As of press time, Brent crude oil futures were trading at $88.38 per barrel.

Previously, in the early days of the US-Iran conflict, Brent crude oil rose to more than $126 per barrel at the end of April. This month, due to the renewed conflict between the United States and Iran, the global energy market was affected, and the price of Brent crude oil once rebounded to above US$91 per barrel.

This week, the Houthi armed forces in Yemen also officially announced an immediate naval blockade of Saudi Arabia, a move that may further exacerbate the problem of oil supply disruptions.

Analysts said that while lower global inventories in the second quarter made the oil market more vulnerable to supply shocks, lower Chinese imports and more elastic demand could limit expected gains.

In terms of investment advice, for investors who want to hedge against ongoing geopolitical shocks in the Middle East and Russia, Goldman Sachs recommends going long on the long-distance price difference of European diesel from December 2026 to March 2027.

Such transactions usually bet on the expansion or maintenance of high diesel price differences between different delivery months. Analysts explained that the diesel market was already in a tense state before the war. First, Ukraine continued to attack Russian refineries.

Second, factors such as hurricanes, extreme high temperatures in summer, and delayed refinery maintenance were adding additional risks to the supply side.

Just in: Morning News; The United States announced a 50% tariff on some Canadian products; short bets on US stocks are approaching historical highs, and the financial reports of technology giants are set.

Summary: The United States announced a 50% tariff on some Canadian products; short bets on U.S. stocks are approaching historical highs, and technology giants’ financial reports set the direction; the Hong Kong Stock Exchange said that extending trading hours is still in early stages of discussion. U.S. stock market: The three major U.S.

stock indexes closed down across the board on July 20.

As of the close, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 307.16 points from the previous trading day to close at 51839.26 points, a decrease of 0.59%; the S&P 500 stock index fell 14.41 points to close at 7443.28 points, a decrease of 0.19%; the Nasdaq Composite Index fell 12.17 points to close at 25508.07 points, a decrease of 0.05%.

Large technology stocks rose or fell, with Microsoft and Intel rising by more than 2%, Broadcom rising by nearly 2%, Google, Amazon, and AMD rising by more than 1%, and Nvidia rising slightly. Apple fell by more than 2%, Tesla fell by nearly 3%, SpaceX fell by more than 3%, and Oracle fell by nearly 4%. Optical communications and storage sectors mostly rose.

Lumentum and Credo rose by more than 4%, Marvell Technology rose by more than 3%, Coherent, SanDisk, and Western Digital rose by more than 2%. Seagate Technology and Micron Technology rose by nearly 2%; SK Hynix fell by nearly 2%.

Cryptocurrency concept stocks rose sharply, with Bit Digital rising by 10%, Galaxy Digital rising by more than 9%, and Circle and Bakkt rising by more than 8%. Most popular Chinese concept stocks rose, with the Nasdaq China Golden Dragon Index rising 0.9%.

Alibaba rose more than 4%, Tencent Holdings ADR, iQiyi, https://t.co/6YYIAsV2GU, and Weibo rose more than 3%, and Baidu, NetEase, Pinduoduo, and Bilibili rose more than 2%. European stock market: Europe's three major stock indexes had mixed gains and losses on July 20.

The Financial Times average price index of 100 stocks in the London stock market closed at 10524.76 points, down 75.61 points or 0.71% from the previous trading day; the CAC40 index in the Paris stock market in France closed at 83 40.11 points, an increase of 1.3 points, or 0.02%, from the previous trading day; the DAX index of the Frankfurt stock market in Germany closed at 24846.69 points, an increase of 15.71 points, or 0.06%, from the previous trading day.

Commodity market: International oil prices rose on July 20. As of the close of the day, the continuous contract of WTI crude oil futures on the New York Mercantile Exchange for the month rose by US$0.64 to close at US$82.42 per barrel, an increase of 0.78%.

The continuous contract of COMEX gold futures for the month fell by US$7.00, or 0.17%, to US$4,011.8 per ounce. The continuous contract of COMEX silver futures for the month rose by US$0.34, or 0.60%, to US$56.665 per ounce. Spot gold fell by $9.83, or 0.24%, to $4,007.77 per ounce. Spot silver rose $0.46, or 0.82%, to $56.41 per ounce.

Trump said Iran would pay the price for killing American soldiers, and senior Iranian officials said the mediator proposed a 10-day ceasefire between Iran and the United States. According to foreign media reports on the 20th, citing a senior Iranian official, the Iran-US negotiation mediator has made a proposal to Iran aimed at easing the current situation.

The proposal recommends the implementation of a 10-day ceasefire in order to seek to resume the implementation of the memorandum of understanding reached by Iran and the United States last month.

Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Bagaei said at a press conference that day that Iran has received suggestions from the negotiation mediators between the United States and Iran, and the specific details cannot be disclosed for the time being.

He also said that the Iranian Interior Minister will visit Pakistan that day to discuss matters related to the two countries. Trump posted on Truth Social: "Every time Iran kills an American soldier, they will pay the price many times over!

This directive has been conveyed to Secretary of War Pete Hegseth, Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Daniel Cain, and every leader in the military." After the United States confirmed that at least three soldiers were killed in the recent battle with Iran, Brent crude oil surged nearly 4% overnight, exceeding $90 per barrel.

Oil prices retreated after Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Ismail Baghaei said negotiations with the United States could be based on Tehran's interests. Trump signs new proclamation imposing 50% tariff on some Canadian goods On Monday, U.S.

President Trump signed an announcement deciding to impose a 50% new tariff on some Canadian goods in accordance with Section 338 of the Tariff Act of 1930.

This measure will take effect in 30 days; energy, potash fertilizers, fish, critical minerals, as well as automobiles and metals and other commodities that have been separately levied with industry tariffs are not included in this increase. It is said that the new tariffs will take effect from 00:01 a.m.

Eastern Time on August 19, and will be levied on top of existing relevant tariffs, taxes, and other charges. The official emphasized that products under the existing North American Trade Agreement between the United States, Canada and Mexico will not receive any exemptions this time.

The lack of this exemption means that a large number of Canadian goods currently flowing across the border duty-free will face high tariffs. The head of U.S.

President Trump's administration's artificial intelligence security agency resigns According to Axios, Chris Fall, director of the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI), the agency responsible for AI testing and standard setting under the U.S. Department of Commerce, announced his resignation only three months after taking office.

The organization, formerly known as the American AI Safety Institute, is responsible for the testing, evaluation and standard support of advanced AI systems.

Fall was appointed director in April following an agency reorganization, and his sudden departure will leave the key federal AI office without permanent leadership as the Trump administration debates next steps for AI safety and regulation. Behind the correction of U.S.

stocks lies a fierce game: the level of short bets hits a record The latest statistics show that short bets on the U.S. stock market have soared to the highest level since statistics began. This may be part of the reason why technology bull stocks have pulled back recently.

According to data collected by S3 Partners since 2010, short positions in S&P 500 stocks as a percentage of free float are currently approaching 3.79%, just one step away from the highest level in history. At the same time, the proportion of short shares in the Russell 3000 Index recently rose to 6.3%, which has set a record.

Data from Goldman Sachs also shows that the short position of the median S&P 500 component has reached 3% of its market capitalization, the highest level since late 2011. Overall short positions in the S&P 500 have grown nearly 10% in the past three months and now stand at about 1.4 billion shares. In the first six months of this year, shorting U.S.

stocks was basically a money-losing business. But as talk of AI-related capital spending heats up, bears appear to have found a reason to hang on. The "positive gamma" buffer of U.S. stock options has weakened significantly, and market fluctuations may be amplified After the expiration of U.S.

stock options in July, the "positive gamma shock absorber" that stabilized the market has basically disappeared: previously, options institutions would buy down and sell up to buffer the market. Now this force has weakened significantly, and U.S. stock fluctuations will be significantly amplified.

The early decline was only concentrated in the semiconductor sector, and funds were diverted to head technology and medical to stabilize the market. Now the risk of a chip correction may spread to the entire market. This week's financial report is the key to determining the trend.

Technology giants such as Google-C (https://t.co/e3Mcns7ywK), Tesla (https://t.co/gLZxiYPP4t), Intel (https://t.co/cRlFNwH0hU) and other technology giants have concentrated on disclosing their performance. The expected range of rise or fall of a single stock is as high as 13%.

In the absence of a shock-absorbing mechanism, the rise and fall of individual stocks are more likely to cause violent fluctuations in the market. The key watershed of the S&P 500 is 7,480 points. If it falls below it, it will weaken, and it will stabilize and become stronger.

In subsequent operations, you need to be wary of sharp rises and falls, and focus on the quality of technology financial reports.

Hong Kong Stock Exchange responds to rumors of "extension of trading hours": very early stage of discussion On July 20, in response to market rumors that "the Hong Kong Exchange is considering extending stock trading hours and canceling the lunch break," the Hong Kong Exchange told the Beijing News Shell Finance reporter that the Hong Kong Exchange has been

committed to improving Hong Kong's competitiveness as an international financial center and is currently studying a number of measures to improve market convenience, including extending trading hours. "Our current priority is to study the proposal to extend the trading hours of the derivatives market.

Whether to implement the relevant proposals is subject to the collection of market opinions and regulatory approval." The Hong Kong Stock Exchange pointed out. The Hong Kong Exchanges and Clearing Limited said proposals to optimize trading hours in the stock spot market are still at a very early stage of discussion.

Any adjustments to the spot market require careful assessment of the impact on the market, and comprehensive consideration of the opinions of all market parties and the arrangements for the interconnection mechanism.

South Korea’s ruling party calls for strengthening the supervision of single-stock leveraged ETFs and introducing further measures if necessary According to South Korea's "Seoul Economic Daily", on the 20th, South Korea's Democratic Party urged the Financial Services Commission (FSC) to focus on monitoring single-stock leveraged ETFs and formulate supplementary measures when necessary.

On the same day, members of the Democratic Party of Korea, affiliated with the Political Affairs Committee of the National Assembly, held a party and government symposium with the Financial Committee at the National Assembly in Yeouido, Seoul, and listened to work reports from Financial Committee Chairman Lee Eok-won and other officials.

Park Sang-hyuk, the ruling party secretary of the National Assembly Political Affairs Committee and a member of the Democratic Party of Korea, told reporters after the meeting: "The Financial Committee reported on the relevant measures announced last Thursday.

Members generally requested regulatory authorities to further strengthen market monitoring and situation analysis, and if necessary, more supplementary measures should be studied and introduced." BlackRock plans to issue more than $12 billion in bonds to finance Meta Texas data center In May of this year, it was reported that the total financing scale of the data center project Project Sopaipilla Holdings may reach approximately US$13 billion.

Project Sopaipilla is 80% owned by funds managed by Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP) and HPS Investment Partners, both currently subsidiaries of BlackRock (https://t.co/4OFd94OSvu), while Meta Platforms (https://t.co/2jAnLeffft) holds the remaining 20%.

People familiar with the matter said the issuer has tasked JPMorgan Chase and Morgan Stanley to organize a conference call with fixed-income investors on Wednesday and is expected to complete pricing early next week.

Meta previously stated that the 1 gigawatt (GW) data center is expected to be operational in 2028 and will provide more than 300 on-site jobs upon completion.

The financing of this data center project will further promote the bond issuance boom this year around the construction of global AI infrastructure, and a large amount of debt financing is also increasing the pressure on bond valuations in the technology industry.

Oracle's credit risk rises to nearly 18-year high, and the market increases its debt burden The cost of insuring Oracle's (ORCL) debt against default rose to a multi-year high on Monday, while its existing bonds sold off, casting doubt on whether its massive investments in artificial intelligence will pay off.

The five-year credit default swap (CDS) annualized rate on the company's debt rose to about 2.03 percentage points in early trading on Monday, according to ICE Data Services. That's the highest level since records began in late 2008, surpassing Friday's peak of 198.23 basis points.

At the same time, Oracle bond prices across all maturities were generally weaker. The spread on one of the company's most actively traded bonds - a 6.7% bond due in 2056 - widened about 8 basis points to 263 basis points, while the spread on a 5.7% bond due in 2036 also widened about 9 basis points to 205 basis points, according to TRACE data.

Google is revealed to be building a new AI server chip with the goal of significantly improving Gemini operating efficiency The latest news shows that Google-C (https://t.co/e3Mcns7ywK) / Google-A (https://t.co/Ct0XkhllaP) is developing a new server chip internally codenamed "Frozen v2", which is designed to run the Gemini model more efficiently.

According to reports, this chip will permanently embed part of the Gemini model architecture into the chip, thereby reducing the amount of calculation and data transmission required to answer user questions.

According to reports, Google plans to deploy the Frozen chip in 2028, but it will not replace the general-purpose TPU, but will become a more targeted and dedicated branch of Google's customized chip product line.

Microsoft to deploy AMD's Helios rack-scale solution on its Azure cloud platform On July 20, Microsoft (https://t.co/dVGi0ITc2W) will deploy AMD's Helios rack-scale solution on its Azure cloud platform to drive cutting-edge model AI inference workloads.

Futurum Group estimates that in the most comprehensive cloud cooperation agreement with Microsoft to date, the Helios rack-level AI system was purchased by Microsoft at a price about 40% higher than Nvidia Rubin's full-stack purchase, marking a fundamental change in AMD's competitive logic in the AI infrastructure market.

Microsoft will deploy Helios on Azure for cutting-edge AI inference, and introduce the sixth-generation EPYC Venice CPU virtual machine series and Pensando DPU. This is the first time AMD has implemented the full stack of "GPU+CPU+network" in a single cloud customer.

Air taxi company turns into military industry player, Archer Aviation stock price skyrockets On Monday (July 20), Archer Aviation announced on its official website that the company and defense technology company Anduril released a vertical take-off and landing aircraft (VTOL) platform.

The research and development of this platform is based on the cooperation agreement reached by the two parties in 2024 and can be applied in both commercial and military fields.

Anduril also released the "Thunder", an autonomous attack rotorcraft based on this platform at the Farnborough International Air Show that day, designed to cooperate with current and next-generation manned attack aircraft and assault aircraft. Affected by this news, Archer Aviation (https://t.co/QbrrnCa8a6) stock price rose nearly 20% during the day.

Wedbush is bullish before Intel results: Q2 results are expected to be strong, but market sentiment is still the biggest variable The veteran American chip manufacturing giant Intel (https://t.co/cRlFNwH0hU) will announce its second-quarter earnings after the U.S. stock market closes on July 23, Eastern Time.

Wedbush analyst Matt Bryson wrote in a note to clients: "Operations may benefit in the third quarter as both revenue and margins look set to easily beat second-quarter expectations, and a similar scenario is likely to follow in the third quarter (after the company previously implemented another layoff plan).

expenses), we believe that the market's performance expectations will be significantly increased." Looking forward to the upcoming financial report, Bryson believes that the data center business may become the main driving force for Intel's quarterly performance growth, and the sales of this business are expected to increase by 10% month-on-month and 40% year-on-year.

He said the company may also benefit from increased pricing power as average selling prices (ASPs) posted double-digit percentage growth in the quarter. SpaceX rocket launch failed again, aborted at the last minute for the second time recently SpaceX (https://t.co/KVvnlZASkf) closed down 3.3% on Monday local time, with its stock price hitting a new low since its listing.

SpaceX planned to launch the Falcon 9 rocket on July 20 to carry out the mission of transporting 24 Starlink satellites. However, the launch rarely failed. When the countdown reached 0 seconds, the engine ignited and did not launch. The live broadcast narration immediately announced that the launch plan had been cancelled.

This is the second time in recent history that the launch mission has been suspended. Spacex later announced that it would suspend Starlink launches from now on to ensure safety. In addition, SpaceX announced that it will announce its financial and operating results for the second quarter of 2026 on August 4.

Last week, SpaceX's "Starship" rocket was originally scheduled to conduct its 13th test flight from Texas, but it triggered an automatic abort procedure one second before liftoff.

Top Stories

On the morning of July 20, Wu Qing, Chairman of the China Securities Regulatory Commission, conducted a field visit to a securities branch in Beijing and chaired an investor symposium, engaging in face-to-face exchanges with eight representatives including large, medium, small, and retail investors to solicit opinions and suggestions on promoting the stable and healthy development of the capital market. Wu Qing stated that investors are the foundation of the market and the most important participants in the capital market. The CSRC will adhere to an integrated approach to prevent risks, strengthen regulation, and promote high-quality development of the capital market, fully safeguarding market stability, enhancing the transparency and authenticity of listed companies, delivering better returns to investors, urging industry institutions to operate in compliance and improve investor services, continuously improving long-term investor protection mechanisms, and resolutely maintaining an open, fair, and just market order to enable investors to better share the fruits of high-quality economic and capital market development.

The State Council Information Office held a press conference to introduce the development of industry and information technology in the first half of the year. The Ministry of Industry and Information Technology stated that it will issue and implement industry-specific and thematic 15th Five-Year Plan guidelines for the industrial and information technology sector; continue implementing new rounds of growth-stabilization action plans for industries such as machinery and automobiles to comprehensively expand high-quality supply and effective demand; will issue guidelines for the construction of computing power standard systems and promote the establishment of standards for market-based computing power pricing; accelerate the cultivation of emerging growth points such as photonics and spatiotemporal information and speed up the implementation of integrated computing-power layouts; will expedite the issuance of the "AI + Software" action plan.

U.S. forces launched a new strike against Iran. President Trump posted on social media, suggesting that U.S. forces would launch a strong retaliatory action against Iran following recent casualties. The Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps stated that it struck U.S. military targets in Bahrain and Kuwait in three phases. A senior Iranian official said mediators have proposed a 10-day ceasefire to discuss reviving the temporary agreement between Iran and the United States. Iranian Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Baghaei confirmed receipt of the mediator's proposal, stating that negotiations with the United States depend on whether they align with Iran's national interests.

Britain has seen its seventh prime minister in ten years. Andy Burnham, newly elected leader of the ruling Labour Party, was appointed by the British monarch as the new prime minister and authorized to form a new cabinet. 

Andy Burnham delivered a speech pledging to end a decade of political turmoil in Britain, implement the "largest reform in 40 years," and reshape the country with a new economic model, with a ten-year economic recovery plan to be released before the end of this year.

China Macro & Policy

Premier Li Qiang presided over a State Council Executive Meeting, hearing reports on inspections of the expansion and quality improvement of the service sector and the planning and construction of the "Six Networks"—water networks, new power grids, computing power networks, next-generation communication networks, urban underground utility networks, and logistics networks. The meeting approved the "15th Five-Year Plan for Intellectual Property Protection and Utilization," studied relevant work on optimizing the social security system, and heard recommendations on rectifying issues identified in the 2025 audit of central budget execution and other fiscal revenues and expenditures. The meeting emphasized the need to systematically advance the planning and construction of the "Six Networks," accelerate implementation according to local conditions and industry needs, strengthen financial and elemental support, and ensure the effective construction of major infrastructure networks.

Zheng Shanjie, Chairman of the National Development and Reform Commission, presided over a private enterprise symposium to solicit opinions and suggestions on the economic situation in the first half of the year and economic work for the second half. Zheng stated that the NDRC will leverage the combined effect of existing and new policies, accelerate the cultivation of new consumption growth points, continuously promote the implementation of major projects outlined in the 15th Five-Year Plan outline, facilitate smooth transitions between old and new growth drivers, accelerate the development and opening of application scenarios, further build a high-quality data supply system, and deepen the construction of a unified national market.

Lin Jian, spokesperson for the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, presided over a regular press briefing. Lin stated that summit diplomacy plays an irreplaceable strategic guiding role in China-U.S. relations, and both sides have maintained communication regarding summit interactions this year.

A symposium for leading officials of industrial and information technology authorities across the country was held in Beijing. The meeting emphasized strengthening legislative research and reserves in emerging fields such as digital economy, artificial intelligence, and low-altitude economy, intensifying efforts to rectify "cutthroat" competition, and continuously fostering a first-class business environment characterized by marketization, rule of law, and internationalization.

The Ministry of Commerce convened a national video conference to advance mid-year work on the policy for replacing old consumer goods with new ones. The meeting stressed the need to implement the policy meticulously, further intensify efforts to expand its coverage, optimize the subsidy review and disbursement process, facilitate public participation and benefit, and enhance public satisfaction.

The State Administration for Market Regulation issued the "Action Plan for Promoting Platform Enterprises to Empower Individual Businesses," proposing that by the end of 2027, a series of "five batches" of achievements will be continuously formed to explore long-term practical pathways for platform enterprises to empower individual businesses. The Action Plan deploys five special actions: startup support, key cultivation, capability enhancement, digital empowerment, and innovation demonstration.

The PBOC announced the July LPR rates: 3% for the one-year term and 3.5% for terms longer than five years, unchanged from June, in line with market expectations. Since the 10-basis-point cut in May 2025, the LPR has remained unchanged for 14 consecutive months. Although the July LPR held steady, the PBOC has repeatedly emphasized the implementation of a moderately loose policy. Industry experts generally anticipate a possible 5- to 10-basis-point reduction in the second half of the year, primarily based on room left by the PBOC's moderately loose policy, potential declines in banks' liability costs, and possible underperformance of economic data or the real estate market.

Data released by the National Bureau of Statistics showed that in June, the unemployment rate for urban non-student labor aged 16–24 was 14.9%, down 0.7 percentage points from the previous month; the unemployment rate for urban non-student labor aged 25–29 was 7.1%, down 0.1 percentage point; the unemployment rate for urban non-student labor aged 30–59 was 4.0%, down 0.1 percentage point. Unemployment rates for all three age groups have declined for three consecutive months.

UBS: The probability of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates in July has dropped sharply, but soaring oil prices still make tightening risks difficult to eliminate UBS's latest interest rate strategy report pointed out that U.S.

inflation in June was significantly lower than expected, and the probability of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates in July has dropped significantly. However, the escalating situation in the Middle East and rising oil prices may still push up tightening expectations again.

In June, the overall CPI decreased by 0.42% month-on-month and increased by 3.53% year-on-year; the core CPI decreased by 0.02% month-on-month and increased by 2.59% year-on-year. UBS predicts that core PCE will increase by 0.18% month-on-month and 3.34% year-on-year in June, and determines that May may be the high point of this round of inflation.

After the inflation data was released, the market's pricing for an interest rate hike at the July meeting dropped from a maximum of 11 basis points to less than 3 basis points. UBS expects the Fed to remain on hold in July and to keep interest rates unchanged for the rest of the year as core PCE slows further and employment growth weakens.

Goldman Sachs is telling clients oil could blow past 110 dollars a barrel as the US and Israeli war on Iran chokes Gulf shipping again, and the bank is pricing that possibility like a weather forecast.

Gulf exports had recovered to more than 80 percent of pre-war levels after Washington and Tehran signed a memorandum of understanding in June, but renewed tanker attacks in the Strait of Hormuz have pushed flows below 50 percent, around 11 million barrels a day. Goldman estimates the market is short 13.4 million barrels a day of Persian Gulf supply. The bank holds its Brent forecast at 80 dollars for the fourth quarter of 2026 and 75 for 2027, while warning prices could overshoot 110 if recovery stalls, or drop into the 60s if production rebounds. A reinstated US blockade of Iranian ports could strip another 1.5 to 2 million barrels a day from Iran's exports.

This crisis exists because the United States and Israel launched an air war on Iran in late February 2026, after which Iran's Revolutionary Guard mined the strait and struck merchant tankers in retaliation. A June memorandum briefly reopened flows before fighting resumed. None of that appears in the market note. What appears instead is a blockade of a sovereign nation's ports rendered as a probability input, stripped of the fact that a blockade is itself an act of war.

Who cashes in on the chokepoint: Goldman's trading desks profit from movement, and a 50 dollar spread between downside and upside scenarios is itself a tradable asset. Gulf monarchies hosting US military infrastructure reroute crude through the Red Sea and collect a scarcity premium. Iranian civilians and stranded tanker crews absorb the actual cost of a war fought to police who sells energy from the Gulf.

My take: Wall Street has turned a war on Iran into a forecasting exercise with no casualties in the model. Goldman's note treats mined shipping lanes and blockaded ports as probability inputs, never as the outcome of a US and Israeli decision to bomb Iran in February. The bank's numbers—an 80 point swing in export flows, a 13.4 million barrel shortfall, a 50 dollar price band—describe a war economy dressed as a supply chain problem. Every dollar Brent climbs is a transfer from ordinary drivers and Iranian civilians to institutions structuring bets on both directions at once.

2026年7月20日盘后分析

美股进入关键转折周：宏观风险叠加财报考研，机构策略重心正在转向

2026年7月20日，美股步入了一个至关重要的时间节点。本周不仅面临美联储政策动向的持续影响，更有包括谷歌（Google）和特斯拉（Tesla）在内的多项科技巨头财报密集出炉。这些财报不仅将决定个股走势，更将考验目前相对脆弱的AI板块，尤其是AI硬件领域的韧性。

一、 宏观警报：地缘局势推动通胀风险重燃

目前市场最真金白银的风险信号来自于霍尔木兹海峡。受地缘冲突升级影响，航运保险公司已将该地区的战争险保费率推升至8%至10%，这达到了本轮冲突爆发以来的最高水平。

这种局势的恶化直接导致原油价格一度逼近90美元，并存在重回100美元大关的可能。对于美联储而言，这无疑是“致命打击”，可能引发加息预期的再度升温，从而使处于高位震荡的股指面临下行回落的巨大风险。

二、 机构策略转向：“重个股、轻大盘”的分散度交易

高盛最新的报告指出，目前期权市场的隐含相关性已接近20年来的最低水平。这预示着一个重大转变：分散度交易（Dispersion Trading）将成为年内机构的核心焦点。

这意味着市场走势将更多受个股微观因素驱动，而非宏观大环境。板块和个股之间的分化会愈发剧烈，选股逻辑将比赛道选择更加挑剔。在这一背景下，投资者应优先选择基本面强劲、现金流充足、且估值与技术面仍具上行空间的标的，并对高估值或技术面破位的风险个股及时锁定获利。

三、 AI板块内部分化：资金在轮转而非撤离

尽管AI芯片板块近期波动剧烈，但市场并未出现全面溃败。资金展现出了明显的内部轮转迹象：
资金流向： 从通用AI芯片向定制化芯片（如AVGO、Marvel）、内存芯片及上游设备商转移。
关键博弈： 市场正在屏息以待下周**微软（Microsoft）**的财报，特别是其对AI大规模资本支出合理性的解释，这将是决定AI投资情绪能否延续的关键。

四、 重点个股实战观察

ServiceNow (NOW)： 技术面表现稳健，已成功重回50日和100日均线上方，显示出强劲的反弹动能。期权市场对其财报持明显看涨态度，且其在软件股中具备较好的基本面前景，是目前相对安全的配置目标。
谷歌 (Google/GOOG)： 350美元是关键支撑位和高性价比切入点。目前股价处于低位反弹蓄势阶段，虽然长期基本面无虞，但期权市场情绪偏空，仓位过重者（超过40-50%）需警惕财报后的剧烈震荡。
美国运通 (AXP)： 尽管技术面在高位出现震荡回落信号，但期权市场对其未来一个月的走势极度看涨。由于其服务于高端客户，具备较强的抗风险能力，建议持有者等待财报表现。
防御性板块： 在现价角度，Costco (COST) 比刚破位的沃尔玛 (WMT) 更具吸引力，其底部反弹信号已初现，适合作为避险配置。

五、 总结与展望

当前大盘虽有偏空预期，但尚未出现恐慌性挤兑。板块赛道的持续轮转（如资金从AI硬件转向软件，或在芯片内部切换）起到了稳定器的作用。

接下来的操作核心应放在技术信号的确认上。许多高风险个股（如NBIS、Oracle、SRG等）目前仍处于测试底部或反弹信号尚不明确的阶段，盲目“抄底”风险极高。在当前震荡市中，保持交易纪律、关注个股微观逻辑优于盲目追逐大盘趋势。

CRUDE OIL - WHAT IS NEXT?

I closed 75-80% of my Brent longs (long futures / sold puts / long UZ and VZ spreads) last week and today. Still keeping a decent amount of oil stocks (half - in smaller names, fairly levered to next 2 years prices, but with little or no debt).

Will probably get yelled at again by fellow oil investors :) - like it happened at the end of April.

Anyway, I was buying dips created by "deal is imminent" announcements by Trump & Co since March 9th, reducing risk in $100-120 area and it worked very well, except for the last time (MOU). I started adding way to early in June and had a larger than expected drawdown. While I ultimately made almost all the money back and overall am up quite a lot since the start of the war, this is not meant to be a victory lap - rather the review of what I got wrong.

The "long oil" trade always had two components:

1) geopolitical (Iran's leverage is proportionate to the tightness of the global oil market & maxed before US midterms / they also made no secret of the plan to have some degree of control over Hormuz). This part worked. I was surprised that the market started to price full recovery right after MOU despite vague language (that actually favoured Iran) and lack of real agreement on pretty much any substantial issue (nuclear / Hormuz). Felt wrong.

In reality it turned out that despite clause 5 of the MOU being much closer to "Iran controls the Strait" than to proper freedom of navigation, US had no intention to cede Hormuz (and the scale of US response was harsher than I anticipated). 

Geopolitical logic favouring long trade (at least from today's 87-91 levels) probably still stands, but I do not feel that I have any edge there. It seems that full normalisation is unlikely for quite a while, but there are tons of scenarios other than the escalation trap. The market could stay in limbo for some time - with partial, but still substantial flows or - eventually - after false starts and episodes of hostilities - come some type of arrangement that gives Iran partial, but not full control (joint management with Oman or other Gulf countries, perhaps in exchange to released funds/sanction relief etc). 

It is fairly clear that despite larger than expected escalation the parties are not targeting energy & export infra aggressively - there were some hits, but they were limited. So while hitting a port or a tanker is probably easier than attacking a US base, Iran has shown certain restraint there - which makes me think that the logic by which Iran will end up with full control because they *could* create a very large problem for oil production/exports does not necessarily work in a straightforward way.

I am not an expert there, so do not want to spend much time on that.

2) fundamental angle. Basically my original idea was that the scale of production loss is so large that the crude prices should stay relatively high at least till year end - and trades with breakeven in mid 80s seemed like a no brainer.

While I do not think now that it was/is necessarily wrong, the fundamental case has weakened.

Lots was said about China imports and flexibility of global refining system (managing to concentrate most of run cuts in petchem). There are a couple more factors that I underestimated:

a) Scale of refining bottleneck. There are 3 components there
- RU 2mbd+ refining losses due to drone attacks
- China. It looks like they are able to manage 2.5mbd refinery run cuts with just 600kbd drop in transport fuel production (which in itself reflects substitution). This is probably not sustainable long term as the petchem part of run cuts in part represents final product destocking, but I honestly do not think they will come aggressively bidding for crude at $90+. They also had around 1.9bln barrels between crude and products inventory (as per GS) before the war so could ease some run cuts by drawing  inventory more heavily

covered it here
https://t.co/HEi3hPznYM

- MEG refiners: it looks like product exports recovery could be lagging crude exports recovery - even desite crazy refining margins (see (b))

Add these 3 and it is easy to see how we ended up with record cracks, very high product prices (approaching demand destruction levels) and meh crude. I certainly underestimated the impact and e.g. closed ICE Gasoil spreads way too early leaving 3/4 of the money on the table.

b) Crude export recovery. It seems to me that the pre-MOU assumptions of the  about crude exports recovery were too conservative. 

"Hormuz shuttle" scheme - when risk-taking NOC (UAE mainly), Sinokor and greek tankers shuttle through Hormuz and S2S to more risk averse owners outside of the Strait really makes a difference here. At the same time there is no similar scheme for products - so product exports and MEG refinery outage recovery would be lagging. And the scheme and high risk environment raise freight costs and hurt premiums  but also benchmark crude prices indirectly (remember that China ultimately cares about landed price).

This again ties to refining bottleneck - if we go back to end of June environment with high refinery outages in China / RU /MEG and relatively large crude outflows from the Gulf we could have moderate crude prices even despite still large production outages, very high products and crazy cracks.

Anyway even though the fundamental bull case is certainly not dead (we do have a large inventory draw - EA estimated it at 650mb across products and crude at the end of June - incl. unobservable; I imagine it should be at least 700-750mb by end of July?;  I am sure that a large part of demand reduction is postponed demand, so it will come back), the trade now leans much stronger on geopolitics.

I do not have an angle there + having a very large long position that makes money on escalation screws with your incentives. To be clear - I do not think there is anything morally wrong with being long or short oil, but I really hate war and would rather sleep better when things are more peaceful ....

CHINA DEMAND

IEA report estimates that China demand dropped 1.6mbd YoY in May. Of thouse gasoline is down only 170kbd (but it was projected to drop 120kbd in the baseline pre-war 2026 demand forecast), diesel @ -330kbd vs projected growth of 45pbd. Jet was down 100kbd vs 46kbd projected growth.

Pre-war they had 2025 and 2026 demand growth of roughly 200kbd, so this means 1.8mbd from baseline.

With crude imports drop around 4mbd (vs average of 2025) and refinerly run cuts of 2.5mbd this implies circa 1.5-2mbd between pause in crude stockpiling (1mbd?) and crude draws (another 0.5mbd).  And another 0.7mbd product draws.

I am assuming above that local production growth YoY roughly matches the pre-war baseline demand growth (in reality it was slightly smaller, but within 100kbd).

Then part of the 1.8mbd demand drop - which is mainly concentrated in petchem - is a mix of pause in petchem product stockpiling and petchem products stock draws; ultimately in steady state at least petchem stock draws should reverse.

Overall, it seems to me that Chinese demand drop is much less dramatic, then is often portrayed - most of the adjustment were indeed stockpiling pause and stock draws, but since they were spread between visible and invisible crude storage, product storage (less observable) and petchem product storage (methodologically does not feed oil stock draws, as those are 1 step down the production chain) each component was either not that big or not visible.

Strait of Hormuz and Global Oil Prices
Market report — July 20, 2026

Executive summary
The Strait of Hormuz is presently the dominant geopolitical risk in the oil market. Renewed U.S.–Iran fighting, attacks on commercial vessels, and sharply reduced tanker traffic have pushed Brent crude back above $90 per barrel, up from below $70 following June’s interim ceasefire. The current price therefore contains both a genuine physical-supply shortage and a substantial security premium. Axios, 

Associated Press
The disruption matters disproportionately because Hormuz normally carries roughly one-fifth of global petroleum supply. Alternative pipelines can bypass only a fraction of this volume, so even partial obstruction rapidly affects freight, insurance, inventories, refinery margins, inflation, and consumer fuel prices worldwide.

Why Hormuz matters
The strait is the only maritime exit from the Persian Gulf. Before the present crisis, it handled:
About 20–23 million barrels per day
Roughly 20% of global petroleum consumption
Approximately 29% of worldwide seaborne oil flows
Major exports from Saudi Arabia, Iraq, the UAE, Kuwait, Qatar and Iran

Asia is especially exposed because most Hormuz cargoes travel to China, India, Japan, South Korea and Southeast Asia. Europe is affected through global benchmark pricing and competition for Atlantic Basin barrels; North America is less physically dependent but still pays globally influenced crude and fuel prices. U.S. EIA chokepoint analysis
Saudi and Emirati pipelines can divert some production to the Red Sea and Fujairah, but the EIA estimates only around 2.6 million barrels per day of effective unused bypass capacity—far below normal Hormuz traffic. EIA country analysis

What has happened to prices
The market has experienced several distinct phases:
Initial closure shock: After military action and the de facto closure in early March, tanker movements and Gulf production fell sharply. Brent briefly approached $120 per barrel. IEA March report

Persistent physical disruption: Reduced exports caused inventories to fall, regional production to be shut in, and oil to accumulate in locations unable to ship it. On-land stocks dropped by approximately 170 million barrels in April. IEA May report

Ceasefire relief: June’s interim agreement raised expectations of reopening, bringing Brent below $70.

Renewed escalation: The agreement deteriorated, attacks and blockades resumed, and Brent climbed from approximately $86.57 on July 14 to above $90 on July 19–20. Axios bypass analysis, Axios market update

Volatility has also increased dramatically. The EIA calculates that Brent’s average daily price movement during April and May was about $4 per barrel, compared with $1 during the same period in 2025. EIA petroleum-market assessment

How the shock spreads globally
Physical supply: Tankers cannot safely or economically carry normal volumes. Exporting states eventually reduce production when storage fills, turning a transportation disruption into lost global output.

Freight and insurance: War-risk premiums and scarce willing tanker capacity raise the delivered price even for cargoes that successfully transit. Middle East-to-Asia VLCC rates reached their highest level in EIA records dating to 2005. EIA tanker-rate analysis

Refined products: Diesel, jet fuel and petrochemical feedstocks are particularly exposed. Refiners outside the Gulf must bid for replacement crude, while exported Gulf products become harder to obtain.

Inflation and growth: Higher transportation, electricity, fertilizer and manufacturing costs pass through to consumer prices. Import-dependent emerging economies face the additional burden of weaker currencies and larger trade deficits.

Regional differences: Asian buyers bear the greatest direct replacement cost. Europe competes for West African, North Sea and U.S. barrels. American production cushions the United States, but does not isolate its gasoline and diesel markets from global pricing.

Outlook
ScenarioLikely market directionSecure reopening with sustained tanker trafficRapid removal of the security premium; Brent could fall materially, although depleted inventories would slow normalizationLimited escorted passages and intermittent attacksElevated, highly volatile prices around current levelsProlonged severe restrictionRenewed pressure toward the March highs as inventories fall and Gulf production is shut inDamage to export terminals, pipelines or processing facilitiesPotentially more severe and longer-lasting spike than a shipping-only disruption

The decisive indicator is not a political announcement that the strait is “open,” but the restoration of regular, commercially insured tanker traffic. Until that occurs, oil prices are likely to remain unusually sensitive to individual attacks, negotiations and military developments.

The head of Yemen’s Saudi-backed government said it will begin working to restart oil exports, suspended since late 2022, in a televised address Monday.

🔹Rashad al-Alimi, chairman of the eight-member Presidential Leadership Council based in Aden, said the government would seek to resume exports “by all available means” starting July 20, but did not detail how. Exports were halted after Ansar Allah struck oil terminals in southern Yemen in 2022.

🔹 Before the civil war, Yemen was a minor but steady energy producer with a peak output of over 450,000 barrels per day (bpd) in 2001. Production fell precipitously during the 2015-2022 war. Prior to the late 2022 attacks, Yemen’s total production rebounded only slightly to 45,000 to 50,000 bpd due to war-related damage and lack of investment per OPEC data.

🔹 Today, the actual oil and gas fields are located in three main geographical regions, all of which are held by the internationally recognized government and its allied local factions—not the Ansar Allah.

🔹 The announcement came the same day Ansar Allah declared a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia, opening another potential front as the wider US-Iran war disrupts regional energy routes. The group has demanded a share of Yemen’s oil revenues to pay salaries in areas it controls, a condition the Aden-based government has rejected.
